hub_subtitle: Inglês para o trabalho — do aperto de mão ao acordo fechado, em 7 aulas

lesson.menu_desc: Say who you are and what you do to an international buyer -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 1 -- Introducing Yourself at Work
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ziláudio | Lesson 1 -- Introducing Yourself at Work

listenings.text: Hi Zilaudio, this is Rachel from Chicago. I am back in the office now, and I wanted to say thank you for the visit last week. Your fields are beautiful, and I was very impressed. I talked to my team about your farm, and they have some questions. Could you send me the size of the farm in hectares, and the dates of the harvest? That would help us a lot. I would like to talk again next week, if you have time. Just call me back when you can. Thank you, and have a good day.

Good morning, and welcome to the International Agriculture Fair. The fair opens at nine o'clock, and the doors close at six. Please show your badge at the door. You cannot enter without it. If you are here to meet buyers, go to hall two. That is where all the international companies are today. Hall one is for machinery, and hall three is for seeds and suppliers. There is a talk about the soybean market at eleven o'clock, in the main room. Coffee is free all day, near the entrance. We hope you have a good day, and good business.
